name: Continue
description: The server has received the request headers and the client should proceed to send the request body.

name: Switching Protocols
description: The server agrees to switch protocols as requested by the client, for example upgrading from HTTP/1.1 to WebSockets.

name: OK
description: The request has succeeded. The meaning of the success depends on the HTTP method.

name: Created
description: The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result.

name: Accepted
description: The request has been received but not yet acted upon. The request may or may not be eventually acted upon.

name: Non-Authoritative Information
description: The returned metadata is not exactly the same as is available from the origin server, but is collected from a local or a third-party copy.

name: No Content
description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

name: Partial Content
description: This response code is used when the Range header is sent from the client to request only part of a resource.

name: Multi-Status
description: Conveys information about multiple resources, for situations where multiple status codes might be appropriate (WebDAV).

name: Moved Permanently
description: The URL of the requested resource has been changed permanently. The new URL is given in the response.

name: Found
description: The URI of the requested resource has been changed temporarily. The client should continue to use the original URI.

name: Not Modified
description: The response has not been modified, so the client can continue to use the same cached version of the response.

name: Temporary Redirect
description: The server sends this response to direct the client to get the requested resource at another URI with the same method that was used in the prior request.

name: Permanent Redirect
description: The resource is now permanently located at another URI. This is similar to 301 but the client must not change the HTTP method used in the request.

name: Bad Request
description: The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (malformed request syntax, invalid message framing, etc.).

name: Unauthorized
description: Although the HTTP standard specifies 'unauthorized', semantically this response means 'unauthenticated'. The client must authenticate itself to get the requested response.

name: Forbidden
description: The client does not have access rights to the content; that is, it is unauthorized, so the server is refusing to give the requested resource.

name: Not Found
description: The server cannot find the requested resource. The URL is not recognized.

name: Method Not Allowed
description: The request method is known by the server but is not supported by the target resource.

name: Request Timeout
description: This response is sent on an idle connection by some servers, even without any previous request by the client.

name: Conflict
description: This response is sent when a request conflicts with the current state of the server.

name: Gone
description: This response is sent when the requested content has been permanently deleted from the server, with no forwarding address.

name: Content Too Large
description: Request entity is larger than limits defined by the server; the server might close the connection or return a Retry-After header field.

name: URI Too Long
description: The URI requested by the client is longer than the server is willing to interpret.

name: Unsupported Media Type
description: The media format of the requested data is not supported by the server, so the server is rejecting the request.

name: I'm a teapot
description: The server refuses the attempt to brew coffee with a teapot. This error is a reference to Hyper Text Coffee Pot Control Protocol defined in RFC 2324.

name: Unprocessable Content
description: The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic errors.

name: Too Many Requests
description: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time (rate limiting).

name: Unavailable For Legal Reasons
description: The user agent requested a resource that cannot legally be provided, such as a web page censored by a government.

name: Internal Server Error
description: The server has encountered a situation it does not know how to handle.

name: Not Implemented
description: The request method is not supported by the server and cannot be handled. The only methods that servers are required to support are GET and HEAD.

name: Bad Gateway
description: The server, while working as a gateway to get a response needed to handle the request, got an invalid response.

name: Service Unavailable
description: The server is not ready to handle the request. Common causes are a server that is down for maintenance or that is overloaded.

name: Gateway Timeout
description: This error response is given when the server is acting as a gateway and cannot get a response in time.

name: Insufficient Storage
description: The method could not be performed on the resource because the server is unable to store the representation needed to successfully complete the request (WebDAV).
